logic_config.trigger_assertion: first two hs-cTn measurements of the 0 h/1 h algorithm are inconclusive and no alternative diagnoses explaining the condition have been made

text: Additional testing after 3 h is recommended if the first two hs-cTn measurements of the 0 h/1 h algorithm are inconclusive and no alternative diagnoses explaining the condition have been made.

logic_config.required_artifact: Emergency TTE
logic_config.trigger_assertion: suspected ACS presenting with cardiogenic shock or suspected mechanical complications

text: Emergency TTE is recommended in patients with suspected ACS presenting with cardiogenic shock or suspected mechanical complications.

logic_config.trigger_condition: suspected ACS
logic_config.forbidden_procedure: Routine, early coronary computed tomography angiography

text: Routine, early coronary computed tomography angiography in patients with suspected ACS is not recommended.

logic_config.required_artifact: regional networks for expeditious and effective STEMI reperfusion therapy, maximizing PPCI availability

text: It is recommended that the pre-hospital management of patients with a working diagnosis of STEMI is based on regional networks designed to deliver reperfusion therapy expeditiously and effectively, with efforts made to make PPCI available to as many patients as possible.

logic_config.required_artifact: 24/7 PPCI service with immediate availability

text: It is recommended that PPCI-capable centres deliver a 24/7 service and are able to perform PPCI without delay.
It is recommended that patients transferred for PPCI bypass the emergency department and CCU/ICU and are transferred directly to the catheterization laboratory.
Oxygen is recommended in patients with hypoxaemia (SaO2 <90%).

logic_config.required_artifact: transfer to a PCI-capable centre, bypassing non-PCI centres
logic_config.trigger_assertion: suspected STEMI

text: It is recommended that EMS transfer patients with suspected STEMI to a PCI-capable centre, bypassing non-PCI centres.

logic_config.required_artifact: ambulance teams trained and equipped to identify ECG patterns suggestive of acute coronary occlusion and to administer initial therapy, including defibrillation and fibrinolysis

text: It is recommended that ambulance teams are trained and equipped to identify ECG patterns suggestive of acute coronary occlusion and to administer initial therapy, including defibrillation, and fibrinolysis when applicable.

logic_config.required_artifact: process for recording and auditing delay times for suspected STEMI patients

text: It is recommended that all hospitals and EMS participating in the care of patients with suspected STEMI record and audit delay times and work together to achieve and maintain quality targets.
Routine oxygen is not recommended in patients with oxygen saturation >90%.

logic_config.required_artifact: Reperfusion therapy recommendation
logic_config.trigger_assertion: working diagnosis of STEMI (persistent ST-segment elevation or equivalents) AND symptoms of ischaemia of ≤12 h duration

text: Reperfusion therapy is recommended in all patients with a working diagnosis of STEMI (persistent ST-segment elevation or equivalents) and symptoms of ischaemia of ≤12 h duration.
A PPCI strategy is recommended over fibrinolysis if the anticipated time from diagnosis to PCI is <120 min.

logic_config.required_artifact: fibrinolytic therapy within 12 hours of symptom onset
logic_config.trigger_assertion: timely PPCI (<120 min) cannot be performed in patients with a working diagnosis of STEMI

text: If timely PPCI (<120 min) cannot be performed in patients with a working diagnosis of STEMI, fibrinolytic therapy is recommended within 12 h of symptom onset in patients without

logic_config.required_artifact: Rescue PCI
logic_config.trigger_assertion: failed fibrinolysis (ST-segment resolution <50% within 60–90 min of fibrinolytic administration) OR haemodynamic instability OR electrical instability OR worsening ischaemia OR persistent chest pain

text: Rescue PCI is recommended for failed fibrinolysis (i.e. ST-segment resolution <50% within 60–90 min of fibrinolytic administration) or in the presence of haemodynamic or electrical instability, worsening ischaemia, or persistent chest pain.

logic_config.required_artifact: PPCI strategy
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient has a working diagnosis of STEMI AND time from symptom onset is >12 hours AND (ongoing symptoms suggestive of ischaemia OR haemodynamic instability OR life-threatening arrhythmias)

text: In patients with a working diagnosis of STEMI and a time from symptom onset >12 h, a PPCI strategy is recommended in the presence of ongoing symptoms suggestive of ischaemia, haemodynamic instability, or life-threatening arrhythmias.

logic_config.trigger_condition: STEMI patients presenting >48 h after symptom onset and without persistent symptoms
logic_config.forbidden_treatment: Routine PCI of an occluded IRA

text: Routine PCI of an occluded IRA is not recommended in STEMI patients presenting >48 h after symptom onset and without persistent symptoms.
Transfer to a PCI-capable centre is recommended in all patients immediately after fibrinolysis.

logic_config.required_artifact: Emergency angiography and PCI of the IRA
logic_config.trigger_assertion: new-onset or persistent heart failure/shock after fibrinolysis

text: Emergency angiography and PCI of the IRA, if indicated is recommended in patients with new-onset or persistent heart failure/shock after fibrinolysis.

logic_config.target: Angiography and PCI of the IRA

text: Angiography and PCI of the IRA, if indicated, is recommended between 2 and 24 h after successful fibrinolysis.

logic_config.required_artifact: invasive strategy during hospital admission
logic_config.trigger_assertion: NSTE-ACS patients with high-risk criteria or with a high index of suspicion for unstable angina

text: An invasive strategy during hospital admission is recommended in NSTE-ACS patients with high-risk criteria or with a high index of suspicion for unstable angina.

logic_config.required_artifact: selective invasive approach
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient without very high- or high-risk features and a low index of suspicion for NSTE-ACS

text: A selective invasive approach is recommended in patients without very high- or high-risk features and a low index of suspicion for NSTE-ACS.

logic_config.required_artifact: immediate invasive strategy
logic_config.trigger_assertion: NSTE-ACS diagnosis with very high-risk criteria (haemodynamic instability, cardiogenic shock, or recurrent/refractory chest pain)

text: An immediate invasive strategy is recommended in patients with a working diagnosis of NSTE-ACS and with at least one of the following very high-risk criteria: • Haemodynamic instability or cardiogenic shock • Recurrent or refractory chest pain despite medical treatment

logic_config.required_artifact: Aspirin therapy

text: Aspirin is recommended for all patients without contraindications at an initial oral LD of 150–300 mg (or 75–250 mg i.v.) and an MD of 75–100 mg o.d. for long-term treatment.

logic_config.treatment_regimen: initial oral loading dose (LD) followed by a maintenance dose (MD) for 12 months
logic_config.trigger_assertion: Patient diagnosed with Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS)

text: In all ACS patients, a P2Y12 receptor inhibitor is recommended in addition to aspirin, given as an initial oral LD followed by an MD for 12 months unless there is high bleeding risk.

logic_config.required_artifact: proton pump inhibitor in combination with dual antiplatelet therapy
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patients at high risk of gastrointestinal bleeding

text: A proton pump inhibitor in combination with dual antiplatelet therapy is recommended in patients at high risk of gastrointestinal bleeding.
Prasugrel is recommended in P2Y12 receptor inhibitor-naïve patients proceeding to PCI (60 mg LD, 10 mg o.d. MD, 5 mg o.d. MD for patients aged ≥75 years or with a body weight <60 kg).

logic_config.context: irrespective of treatment strategy (invasive or conservative)

text: Ticagrelor is recommended irrespective of the treatment strategy (invasive or conservative) (180 mg LD, 90 mg twice a day MD).

logic_config.required_artifact: Clopidogrel administration
logic_config.trigger_assertion: prasugrel or ticagrelor are unavailable, not tolerated, or contraindicated

text: Clopidogrel (300–600 mg LD, 75 mg o.d. MD) is recommended when prasugrel or ticagrelor are not available, cannot be tolerated, or are contraindicated.

logic_config.required_artifact: DAPT resumed for at least 12 months after CABG surgery
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient with ACS stops DAPT for CABG

text: If patients presenting with ACS stop DAPT to undergo CABG, it is recommended they resume DAPT after surgery for at least 12 months.

logic_config.forbidden_treatment: pre-treatment with a glycoprotein IIb/IIIa antagonist

text: Pre-treatment with a glycoprotein IIb/IIIa antagonist is not recommended.

logic_config.trigger_condition: NSTE-ACS patients in whom coronary anatomy is not known and early invasive management (<24 h) is planned
logic_config.forbidden_treatment: Routine pre-treatment with a P2Y12 receptor inhibitor

text: Routine pre-treatment with a P2Y12 receptor inhibitor in NSTE-ACS patients in whom coronary anatomy is not known and early invasive management (<24 h) is planned is not recommended.

logic_config.required_artifact: parenteral anticoagulation
logic_config.trigger_assertion: ACS diagnosis

text: Parenteral anticoagulation is recommended for all patients with ACS at the time of diagnosis.

logic_config.required_artifact: UFH bolus
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient undergoing PCI

text: Routine use of a UFH bolus (weight-adjusted i.v. bolus during PCI of 70–100 IU/kg) is recommended in patients undergoing PCI.

logic_config.trigger_condition: patients with STEMI undergoing PPCI

text: Fondaparinux is not recommended in patients with STEMI undergoing PPCI.

logic_config.required_artifact: fondaparinux administration
logic_config.trigger_assertion: NSTE-ACS diagnosis and early invasive angiography not anticipated within 24 hours

text: Patients with NSTE-ACS For patients with NSTE-ACS in whom early invasive angiography (i.e. within 24 h) is not anticipated, fondaparinux is recommended.

logic_config.required_artifact: dual antithrombotic therapy using a non-vitamin K antagonist oral anticoagulant at the recommended dose for stroke prevention and a single oral antiplatelet agent (preferably clopidogrel) for up to 12 months
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patients with atrial fibrillation and CHA2DS2-VASc score ≥1 in men and ≥2 in women, after up to 1 week of triple antithrombotic therapy following the ACS event

text: As the default strategy for patients with atrial fibrillation and CHA 2 DS 2 -VASc score ≥1 in men and ≥2 in women, after up to 1 week of triple antithrombotic therapy following the ACS event, dual antithrombotic therapy using a non-vitamin K antagonist oral anticoagulant at the recommended dose for stroke prevention and a single oral antiplatelet agent (preferably clopidogrel) for up to 12 months is recommended.

logic_config.required_artifact: UFH bolus during PCI
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient is on a NOAC OR INR is <2.5 in VKA-treated patients

text: During PCI, a UFH bolus is recommended in any of the following circumstances: • if the patient is on a NOAC • if the INR is <2.5 in VKA-treated patients.

logic_config.forbidden_treatment: use of ticagrelor or prasugrel as part of triple antithrombotic therapy

text: The use of ticagrelor or prasugrel as part of triple antithrombotic therapy is not recommended.
Discontinuation of antiplatelet treatment in patients treated with an oral anticoagulant is recommended after 12 months.
De-escalation of antiplatelet therapy in the first 30 days after ACS is not recommended.
When fibrinolysis is the reperfusion strategy, it is recommended to initiate this treatment as soon as possible after diagnosis in the pre-hospital setting (aim for target of <10 min to lytic bolus).

logic_config.required_artifact: fibrin-specific agent (tenecteplase, alteplase, or reteplase)

text: A fibrin-specific agent (i.e. tenecteplase, alteplase, or reteplase) is recommended.

logic_config.required_artifact: anticoagulation
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient treated with fibrinolysis
logic_config.duration_condition: until revascularization (if performed) or for the duration of hospital stay (up to 8 days)

text: Anticoagulation is recommended in patients treated with fibrinolysis until revascularization (if performed) or for the duration of hospital stay (up to 8 days).

logic_config.required_artifact: Enoxaparin i.v. followed by subcutaneous as the preferred anticoagulant

text: Enoxaparin i.v. followed by subcutaneous is recommended as the preferred anticoagulant.

logic_config.required_artifact: unfractionated heparin administration (weight-adjusted i.v. bolus, followed by infusion)
logic_config.trigger_assertion: enoxaparin not available

text: When enoxaparin is not available, unfractionated heparin is recommended as a weight-adjusted i.v. bolus, followed by infusion.

logic_config.required_artifact: PPCI strategy
logic_config.trigger_assertion: resuscitated cardiac arrest and an ECG with persistent ST-segment elevation (or equivalents)

text: A PPCI strategy is recommended in patients with resuscitated cardiac arrest and an ECG with persistent ST-segment elevation (or equivalents).

Temperature control (i.e. continuous monitoring of core temperature and active prevention of fever [i.e. >37.7°C]) is recommended after either out-of-hospital or in-hospital cardiac arrest for adults who remain unresponsive after return of spontaneous circulation.

logic_config.trigger_condition: patient is haemodynamically stable and without persistent ST-segment elevation (or equivalents) after resuscitated cardiac arrest

text: Routine immediate angiography after resuscitated cardiac arrest is not recommended in haemodynamically stable patients without persistent ST-segment elevation (or equivalents).

logic_config.required_artifact: transfer to a hospital offering 24/7 PPCI via one specialized EMS
logic_config.trigger_assertion: ACS is suspected after resuscitated cardiac arrest

text: It is recommended that healthcare systems implement strategies to facilitate transfer of all patients in whom ACS is suspected after resuscitated cardiac arrest directly to a hospital offering 24/7 PPCI via one specialized EMS.
Evaluation of neurological prognosis Evaluation of neurological prognosis (no earlier than 72 h after admission) is recommended in all comatose survivors after cardiac arrest.

logic_config.required_artifact: Immediate coronary angiography and PCI of the IRA
logic_config.trigger_assertion: CS complicating ACS

text: Immediate coronary angiography and PCI of the IRA (if indicated) is recommended in patients with CS complicating ACS.

logic_config.trigger_assertion: ACS-related CS and PCI of the IRA is not feasible/unsuccessful

text: Emergency CABG is recommended for ACS-related CS if PCI of the IRA is not feasible/ unsuccessful.

logic_config.required_artifact: emergency surgical/catheter-based repair of mechanical complications of ACS
logic_config.trigger_assertion: haemodynamic instability

text: In cases of haemodynamic instability, emergency surgical/catheter-based repair of mechanical complications of ACS is recommended, based on Heart Team discussion.

logic_config.trigger_condition: ACS patients with CS and without mechanical complications

text: The routine use of an intra-aortic balloon pump in ACS patients with CS and without mechanical complications is not recommended.

logic_config.required_artifact: ICCU/CCU equipped to provide all required aspects of care for high-risk patients

text: It is recommended that all hospitals participating in the care of high-risk patients have an ICCU/CCU equipped to provide all required aspects of care, including treatment of ischaemia, severe heart failure,

logic_config.patient_group: high-risk patients (including all STEMI patients and very high-risk NSTE-ACS patients)

text: It is recommended that high-risk patients (including all STEMI patients and very high-risk NSTE-ACS patients) have ECG monitoring for a minimum of 24 h.

logic_config.patient_criteria: high-risk patients with successful reperfusion therapy and an uncomplicated clinical course (including all STEMI patients and very high-risk NSTE-ACS patients)

text: It is recommended that high-risk patients with successful reperfusion therapy and an uncomplicated clinical course (including all STEMI patients and very high-risk NSTE-ACS patients) are kept in the CCU/ ICCU for a minimum of 24 h whenever possible, after which they may be moved to a step-down monitored bed for an additional 24–48 h.

logic_config.required_artifact: Radial access as the standard approach

text: Radial access is recommended as the standard approach, unless there are over-riding procedural considerations.

logic_config.required_artifact: PCI with stent deployment in the IRA during the index procedure
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient is undergoing PPCI

text: PCI with stent deployment in the IRA during the index procedure is recommended for patients undergoing PPCI.

logic_config.required_artifact: drug-eluting stent
logic_config.trigger_assertion: stent indication

text: Drug-eluting stents are recommended in preference to bare metal stents in all cases.

logic_config.required_artifact: PCI
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient with spontaneous coronary artery dissection AND symptoms and signs of ongoing myocardial ischaemia AND a large area of myocardium in jeopardy AND reduced antegrade flow

text: In patients with spontaneous coronary artery dissection, PCI is recommended only for patients with symptoms and signs of ongoing myocardial ischaemia, a large area of myocardium in jeopardy, and reduced antegrade flow.

logic_config.trigger_condition: routine use

text: The routine use of thrombus aspiration is not recommended.

logic_config.required_artifact: documentation of strategy rationale considering patient clinical status, comorbidities, and disease complexity
logic_config.trigger_assertion: revascularization strategy selected

logic_config.required_artifact: IRA-only PCI during the index procedure

text: IRA-only PCI during the index procedure is recommended.
Complete revascularization is recommended either during the index PCI procedure or within 45 days.

logic_config.required_artifact: angiographic severity assessment
logic_config.trigger_assertion: PCI of non-IRA performed

text: It is recommended that PCI of the non-IRA is based on angiographic severity.

logic_config.forbidden_action: Invasive epicardial functional assessment of non-culprit segments of the IRA

text: Invasive epicardial functional assessment of non-culprit segments of the IRA is not recommended during the index procedure.

logic_config.required_artifact: CMR imaging
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient has working diagnosis of MINOCA AND final diagnosis is not clear after invasive angiography

logic_config.required_artifact: Management plan consistent with underlying diagnosis and disease-specific guidelines
logic_config.trigger_assertion: MINOCA diagnosis established

logic_config.required_artifact: diagnostic algorithm followed
logic_config.trigger_assertion: initial working diagnosis of MINOCA

text: In all patients with an initial working diagnosis of MINOCA, it is recommended to follow a diagnostic algorithm to determine the underlying final diagnosis.
Intravenous beta-blockers are recommended when rate control is needed in the absence of acute HF or hypotension.

logic_config.required_artifact: Intravenous amiodarone administration
logic_config.trigger_assertion: rate control needed in the presence of acute HF and no hypotension

text: Intravenous amiodarone is recommended when rate control is needed in the presence of acute HF and no hypotension.

logic_config.required_artifact: immediate electrical cardioversion
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient has Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS) AND haemodynamic instability AND inadequate rate control with pharmacological agents

logic_config.required_artifact: Intravenous amiodarone administration
logic_config.trigger_assertion: unstable patient with recent-onset AF requiring electrical cardioversion or at risk of early recurrence of AF

logic_config.trigger_condition: contraindication exists

text: Intravenous beta-blocker and/or amiodarone treatment is recommended for patients with polymorphic VT and/or VF unless contraindicated.

logic_config.required_artifact: prompt and complete revascularization
logic_config.trigger_assertion: myocardial ischaemia in patients with recurrent VT and/or VF

text: Prompt and complete revascularization is recommended to treat myocardial ischaemia that may be present in patients with recurrent VT and/or VF.

logic_config.required_artifact: temporary pacing
logic_config.trigger_assertion: failure to respond to atropine

text: • i.v. positive chronotropic medication (adrenaline, vasopressin, and/or atropine) is recommended. • temporary pacing is recommended in cases of failure to respond to atropine. • urgent angiography with a view to

logic_config.required_artifact: implantation of a permanent pacemaker
logic_config.trigger_assertion: high-degree AV block does not resolve within 5 days after MI

text: Implantation of a permanent pacemaker is recommended when high-degree AV block does not resolve within a waiting period of at least 5 days after MI.

logic_config.trigger_condition: high-degree AV block resolves after revascularization or spontaneously

text: Pacing is not recommended if high-degree AV block resolves after revascularization or spontaneously.

logic_config.trigger_condition: asymptomatic and haemodynamically irrelevant ventricular arrhythmias

text: Treatment of asymptomatic and haemodynamically irrelevant ventricular arrhythmias with anti-arrhythmic drugs is not recommended.

logic_config.required_artifact: low- or iso-osmolar contrast media
logic_config.trigger_assertion: invasive strategies are performed

text: The use of low- or iso-osmolar contrast media (at the lowest possible volume) is recommended for invasive strategies.

logic_config.required_artifact: eGFR assessment of kidney function
logic_config.trigger_assertion: diagnosis of ACS

text: It is recommended to assess kidney function using eGFR in all patients with ACS.

logic_config.required_artifact: application of diagnostic and therapeutic strategies consistent with those for patients with normal kidney function, including consideration for dose adjustment
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient has Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD)

text: It is recommended to apply the same diagnostic and therapeutic strategies in patients with CKD (dose adjustment may be necessary) as in patients with normal kidney function.

logic_config.required_artifact: long-term glucose-lowering treatment choice based on comorbidities
logic_config.trigger_assertion: presence of comorbidities (heart failure, CKD, obesity)

text: It is recommended to base the choice of long-term glucose-lowering treatment on the presence of comorbidities, including heart failure, CKD, and obesity.

logic_config.required_artifact: glycaemic status assessment
logic_config.trigger_assertion: initial evaluation in all patients with ACS

text: It is recommended to assess glycaemic status at initial evaluation in all patients with ACS.

logic_config.required_artifact: frequent blood glucose monitoring
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient has known diabetes mellitus OR blood glucose levels are ≥11.1 mmol/L (or ≥200 mg/dL)

text: It is recommended to frequently monitor blood glucose levels in patients with known diabetes mellitus or hyperglycaemia (defined as glucose levels ≥11.1 mmol/L or ≥200 mg/dL).

logic_config.required_artifact: diagnostic and treatment strategies applied uniformly regardless of patient age

text: It is recommended to apply the same diagnostic and treatment strategies in older patients as in younger patients.

logic_config.required_artifact: documented adaptation of antithrombotic and secondary prevention medication choice and dosage
logic_config.trigger_assertion: presence of relevant patient factors (renal function, co-medications, comorbidities, frailty, cognitive function, or specific contraindications)

text: It is recommended to adapt the choice and dosage of antithrombotic agent, as well as of secondary prevention medications, to renal function, co-medications, comorbidities, frailty, cognitive function, and specific contraindications.

logic_config.required_artifact: documentation of individualized interventional and pharmacological treatments based on a holistic approach and risk/benefit evaluation
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient is frail older with comorbidities

text: For frail older patients with comorbidities, a holistic approach is recommended to individualize interventional and pharmacological treatments after careful evaluation of the risks and benefits.

logic_config.required_artifact: invasive strategy
logic_config.trigger_assertion: cancer patients presenting with high-risk ACS with expected life survival ≥6 months

text: An invasive strategy is recommended in cancer patients presenting with high-risk ACS with expected life survival ≥6 months.

logic_config.required_artifact: temporary interruption of cancer therapy
logic_config.trigger_assertion: cancer therapy suspected as contributing cause of ACS

text: A temporary interruption of cancer therapy is recommended in patients in whom the cancer therapy is suspected to be a contributing cause of ACS.
Aspirin is not recommended in cancer patients with a platelet count <10 000/μL.
Clopidogrel is not recommended in cancer patients with a platelet count <30 000/μL.
In ACS patients with cancer and <50 000/μL platelet count, prasugrel or ticagrelor are not recommended.

logic_config.required_artifact: participation in a medically supervised, structured, comprehensive, multidisciplinary exercise-based cardiac rehabilitation and prevention programme

text: It is recommended that all ACS patients participate in a medically supervised, structured, comprehensive, multidisciplinary exercise-based cardiac rehabilitation and prevention programme.

required_artifact: regular aerobic physical activity and resistance exercise
logic_config.trigger_assertion: ACS patient

text: It is recommended that ACS patients adopt a healthy lifestyle, including: • stopping all smoking of tobacco • healthy diet (Mediterranean style) • alcohol restriction • regular aerobic physical activity and resistance exercise • reduced sedentary time

logic_config.required_artifact: initiation or continuation of high-dose statin therapy

text: It is recommended that high-dose statin therapy is initiated or continued as early as possible, regardless of initial LDL-C values.
It is recommended to aim to achieve an LDL-C level of <1.4 mmol/L (<55 mg/dL) and to reduce LDL-C by ≥50% from baseline.

logic_config.required_artifact: addition of a PCSK9 inhibitor
logic_config.trigger_assertion: LDL-C goal not achieved despite maximally tolerated statin therapy and ezetimibe after 4–6 weeks

text: If the LDL-C goal is not achieved despite maximally tolerated statin therapy and ezetimibe after 4–6 weeks, the addition of a PCSK9 inhibitor is recommended

logic_config.required_artifact: addition of ezetimibe
logic_config.trigger_assertion: LDL-C goal is not achieved despite maximally tolerated statin therapy after 4–6 weeks

text: If the LDL-C goal is not achieved despite maximally tolerated statin therapy after 4–6 weeks, the addition of ezetimibe is recommended.

logic_config.required_artifact: intensified lipid-lowering therapy
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient was on lipid-lowering therapy before admission and is in index ACS hospitalization

text: It is recommended to intensify lipid-lowering therapy during the index ACS hospitalization for patients who were on lipid-lowering therapy before admission.

logic_config.required_artifact: Beta-blocker administration or consideration
logic_config.trigger_assertion: ACS diagnosis

text: Beta-blockers Beta-blockers are recommended in ACS patients

logic_config.required_artifact: Angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitors
logic_config.trigger_assertion: ACS patient with HF symptoms, LVEF ≤40%, diabetes, hypertension, and/or CKD

text: Angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitors are recommended in ACS patients with HF symptoms, LVEF ≤40%, diabetes, hypertension, and/or CKD.

logic_config.required_artifact: Mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists recommendation/administration
logic_config.trigger_assertion: ACS patient with LVEF ≤40% and (HF or diabetes)

text: Mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists are recommended in ACS patients with an LVEF ≤40% and HF or diabetes.

logic_config.required_artifact: repeat evaluation of LVEF 6–12 weeks after ACS
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient has pre-discharge LVEF ≤40% after an ACS, complete revascularization, and optimal medical therapy

text: In patients with pre-discharge LVEF ≤40%, repeat evaluation of the LVEF 6–12 weeks after an ACS (and after complete revascularization and the institution of optimal medical therapy) is recommended to assess the potential need for sudden cardiac death primary prevention ICD implantation.

logic_config.required_artifact: Influenza vaccination
logic_config.trigger_assertion: patient is an ACS patient

text: Influenza vaccination is recommended for all ACS patients.

logic_config.required_artifact: patient-centered care approach, incorporating patient preferences, needs, and beliefs into clinical decisions

text: Patient-centred care is recommended by assessing and adhering to individual patient preferences, needs and beliefs, ensuring that patient values are used to inform all clinical decisions.

logic_config.required_artifact: patient involvement in decision-making and informed consent process, including discussion of risks, alternatives, and use of decision aids

text: It is recommended to include ACS patients in decision-making (as much as their condition allows) and to inform them about the risk of adverse events, radiation exposure, and alternative options. Decision aids should be used to facilitate the discussion.

logic_config.required_artifact: symptom assessment using methods that help patients to describe their experience

text: It is recommended to assess symptoms using methods that help patients to describe their experience.

logic_config.anchor: Emergency patient requires resources from other hospital departments
logic_config.target: Resources, equipment, personnel, and services from other hospital departments are available/provided to emergency patient
logic_config.time_criterion: immediate and within timeframes that protect health and safety and meet acceptable standards of practice

text:
(Rev. 37, Issued:  10-17-08; Effective/Implementation Date:  10-17-08)

§482.55(a) - [If emergency services are provided at the hospital --]

- (2) The services must be integrated with other departments of the hospital;

## Interpretive Guidelines §482.55(a)(2)

The hospital's emergency service/department must be integrated with the other departments of the hospital such as surgical services, lab, ICU, diagnostic services, etc. The hospital must demonstrate that its emergency services are truly integrated into its other departments.  The integration must be such that the hospital can immediately make available the full extent of its patient care resources to assess and render appropriate care for an emergency patient.

Emergency Services integration would include at a minimum:

- Coordination and communication between the Emergency Department and other hospital services/departments;
- Physical access for emergency department patients to the services, equipment, personnel, and resources of other hospital departments/services;
- The immediate availability of services, equipment, personnel, and resources of other hospital departments/services to emergency patients; and
- That the provision of services, equipment, personnel and resources of other

hospital departments/services to emergency department patients is within timeframes that protect the health and safety of patients and is within acceptable standards of practice, including:

- o The length of time it takes to transport the emergency patient from the ED to another hospital department where needed interventions or diagnostic services will be rendered.
- o The length of time it takes to deliver equipment or supplies, or for the staff from other departments to travel from their location to the emergency department in order to provide needed interventions, tests, care, or services.

Time is critical in the provision of emergency care.  The hospital must be able to demonstrate how the hospital's other departments provide emergency patients the care and services needed within safe and appropriate times.

In emergency care situations, the time needed to provide the patient with appropriate diagnostic and care interventions can have a significant effect on the patient.  Delays in diagnosis and the provision of needed interventions is likely to adversely affect the health and safety of patients who require emergency care.  Therefore, a hospital that cannot demonstrate integration of its emergency services with its other departments (including radiological services, OR, intensive care, laboratory, etc.) would not be in compliance with the Emergency Services CoP.

Many hospitals offer urgent care services on the hospital campus or in provider-based clinics in the communities they serve.  Those clinics must be in compliance with the hospital CoP.  Hospitals may organize their urgent care clinics as part of their outpatient department or emergency services department.  An urgent care clinic that:

- The hospital holds out to the public as providing only urgent care services and possibly other services;
- Clearly advises the public that the urgent care clinic is not an emergency services department; and
- Does not meet the EMTALA definition of dedicated emergency department;

would be evaluated for compliance with the integration requirement in the Outpatient Services CoP (§482.54(a)) rather than the integration requirement in the Emergency Services CoP.  In most urgent care situations, the time, qualified personnel, equipment, and other resources needed to provide the patient with appropriate diagnostic and care interventions are less than needed in emergency situations.

## Survey Procedures §482.55(a)(2)

Verify that there are established procedures to assure integration with either hospital services including laboratory, radiology, and operating services to provide continuity of care.
